sourceOfTruth: Runtime-ready means a built llama.cpp fork graph route selects the shipped CUDA kernel and a numeric smoke test passes. nvcc compilation and exported symbols are not enough. For turbo3/turbo4 the gate is a built-fork llama-bench run with --cache-type-k tbq3_0/tbq4_0 on real NVIDIA hardware; for qjl/polar/turbo3_tcq (which the fork registers as score-side ops, not --cache-type-k storage aliases — same as the Vulkan path) the gate is make cuda-verify, which ports the same C reference algorithm into __device__ kernels operating on the on-disk fixture byte layouts and matched the references 8/8 on the RTX 5080.
device: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Laptop GPU (Blackwell, compute capability 12.0; driver 580.142, CUDA runtime 13.0). Build: nvcc 12.8 V12.8.93 — real native sm_120a SASS (no longer PTX JIT). Install: ~/.eliza/local-inference/bin/mtp/linux-x64-cuda/ (forkCommit a61c93aaa5 = v1.2.0-eliza, builtAt 2026-05-12T17:16:58Z, libggml-cuda.so.0.9.7 = 473 MB).

turbo3.smokeCommand: make -C packages/inference/verify cuda-verify && llama-bench -ngl 99 --cache-type-k tbq3_0 --cache-type-v tbq3_0 -fa 1
graphRoutes: GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_TBQ/turbo3 (--cache-type-k tbq3_0, backend=CUDA, pp64 1014 t/s on RTX 5080)

turbo4.smokeCommand: make -C packages/inference/verify cuda-verify && llama-bench -ngl 99 --cache-type-k tbq4_0 --cache-type-v tbq4_0 -fa 1
graphRoutes: GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_TBQ/turbo4 (--cache-type-k tbq4_0, backend=CUDA, pp64 1679 t/s on RTX 5080)
GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_TBQ/turbo3_tcq (cuda_verify fixture-parity, sliding 9-bit TCQ window decode, backend=CUDA, RTX 5080)
GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_QJL (cuda_verify fixture-parity, backend=CUDA, RTX 5080; DP4A int8-sketch cross-check 1.43e-1 round-trip, expected)

GGML_OP_FUSED_ATTN_QJL_TBQ (cuda_verify fused_attn_qjl_tbq.json, 4 GQA/n_kv cases n_kv 64/512/256/128 GQA 1/2/4, backend=CUDA, RTX 5080; production cuda/fused-attn-qjl-tbq.cu compiled into the fork's ggml-cuda/ via -DGGML_CUDA_FUSED_ATTN_QJL=ON and patchCudaKernels)
fusedAttn.device: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Laptop GPU (Blackwell sm_120, compute_90 PTX JIT)
fusedAttn.note: Fused QJL-K score -> online softmax -> TBQ3-V mix, score vector never materialized. The standalone cuda/fused-attn-qjl-tbq.cu kernel (P0 shared-mem V-decode + P1 register Q-hoist optimized) compiles into the fork's libggml-cuda via the kernel-patches hook + -DGGML_CUDA_FUSED_ATTN_QJL=ON; the cuda_verify harness ports the same fused algorithm into a __device__ kernel and matched eliza_fused_attn_qjl_tbq3 1920/1920 outputs on the RTX 5080. fused_attn is an optimization on top of the five required kernels (AGENTS.md §3) and is intentionally NOT in requiredRuntimeCapabilityKeys / manifestKernelNames.
